content:
# Debit Dispute Limits Quick Reference

## Open Dispute Capacity by Account Tier
Use the customer's current account tier to determine how many debit-card or electronic-fund-transfer disputes may remain open at the same time.

| Account tier | Maximum open disputes |
| --- | --- |
| Entry | 2 |
| Mid | 3 |
| Premium | 4 |
| Elite | 5 |

Do not open a new dispute when the customer is already at the tier limit. If the customer has several transactions to report, count each open dispute against the limit until it is closed.

## Merchant Contact Requirement
For disputes in the following categories, the customer must first contact the merchant: duplicate_charge, incorrect_amount, goods_services_not_received, recurring_charge_after_cancellation. Ask for the customer's merchant-contact outcome before proceeding with intake for those categories.

This requirement applies to the category, not the transaction amount or card channel. If the case is not in one of the listed categories, do not add a merchant-contact requirement from this quick reference.

## Related Handling Notes
See "Internal: Debit Card Dispute Intake Requirements" for employee intake steps. See "Debit Card Error Notice And Liability" when the customer asks about notice timing or liability rather than open-dispute capacity.
title: Debit Dispute Limits Quick Reference
